name: Tungsten Carbide Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Tungsten Carbide. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Tungsten Carbide oxide layer removal

wavelength.description: Near-infrared wavelength for optimal Tungsten Carbide absorption

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning without thermal damage

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform surface treatment

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for thorough contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for complete surface coverage

power.description: Typical range for industrial cleaning of hard materials like Tungsten Carbide to remove contaminants without ablation; starting at 100-200W to balance efficiency and safety. Max limited to avoid thermal damage given WC's high melting point (~2870°C) and reflectivity at 1064 nm. Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs for cleaning applications; academic review in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018) on metal cleaning thresholds.

scan_speed.description: Effective range for 1064 nm cleaning; slower speeds (500-1500 mm/s) for heavy contamination like oxides, faster (2000-5000 mm/s) for light surface prep. Recommended 2000 mm/s optimizes fluence delivery on WC's conductive surface. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals; case studies in Journal of Cleaner Production (2020) for carbide tool cleaning.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime (5-200 ns) preferred for industrial cleaning of WC to ablate contaminants while minimizing heat-affected zone (HAZ); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precise but costlier. 50 ns balances energy deposition on reflective WC. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) on pulse duration effects for ceramics; avoids fs due to WC's thermal properties.

repetition_rate.description: Typical 10-100 kHz for pulsed cleaning; higher rates increase speed but risk heat accumulation on WC's moderate thermal conductivity (~80 W/m·K). 30 kHz recommended for controlled overlap without overheating. Trade-off: high freq faster but monitor for substrate damage. Source: Academic literature in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2021); Coherent Armonia series specs.

energy_density.description: Fluence range below WC damage threshold (~5-10 J/cm² at 1064 nm) for contaminant removal; 0.5-2 J/cm² for light cleaning, up to 5 for heavy. Recommended 2 J/cm² ensures safe ablation of oxides without melting. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2017) for Tungsten Carbide; conservative for reflectivity reducing absorption.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for cleaning; smaller (50-200 μm) for fine detail on WC tools, larger (200-500 μm) for broad surface prep. 200 μm recommended for uniform coverage on hard, reflective surfaces. Source: Equipment specifications from Adapt Laser Systems; process optimization in Surface and Coatings Technology (2022).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for complete removal of heavy layers on WC; up to 10 for thick coatings. 3 passes recommended for industrial prep to ensure uniformity without excessive exposure. Source: Industry practice in case studies from Journal of Manufacturing Processes (2019); avoids over-processing WC's durable surface.

overlap_ratio.description: Typical 30-80% for uniform scan coverage; 50-70% optimal for WC to prevent gaps in contaminant removal while minimizing heat buildup. 60% balances efficiency and even fluence distribution. Source: Process optimization studies in Optics and Laser Technology (2020); standard in scanning software like those from CleanLaser GmbH.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.citation: A. A. Semerok et al., Journal of Applied Physics, 2001, DOI: 10.1063/1.1352678
laserDamageThreshold.context: Polycrystalline Tungsten Carbide (WC, 99.9% purity), room temperature (25°C), 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, 7 ns pulse duration, measured in vacuum

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds
